titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 759

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=993 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.454131+00 | translation moa=992 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.442823+00

p.cl: Dauid comes . Iohanni episcopo . et Colbano . et Cospatrico . et omnibus suis fidelibus  salutem .

Vos ipsi scitis quod iudicatum fuit
ante me inter monachos Sancti Cuthberti et Drengos meos de terre de Horeuoredane . scilicet quod si ipsi
monachi haberent legales testes . uel breue fratris mei quieta eis illa predicta terra remaneret .

Et ideo
uolo sciatis quod ego ipse uidi breue et donum fratris mei Eadgari regis quod ad uos etiam misi .

Et quicquid illud breue
eis testatur . uolo et concedo ut libere et quiete habeant .
testibus Willelmo nepotis mei . et Osberto capellano . et Hugone de moreuilla .
David the earl to John the bishop, Colban, Cospatric, and all his sworn men sends greeting.

You yourselves know that there was a judgment before me between the monks of Saint Cuthbert and my drengs concerning the land of Horndean, that is to say, that if the monks had legal witnesses, or the brieve of my brother, the aforesaid land should remain theirs unchallenged.
For that reason I want you to know that I have seen the very brieve and gift of my brother, Edgar the king, which I have also sent to you.
And I will and grant that whatever that brieve bears witness in their favour I wish and allow that they have free and unchallenged.
As witness William my nephew, Osbert the chaplain, and Hugh de Morville.
